senses.definition: relating to seborrhea; affecting or resembling seborrhea, a condition causing oily skin or scalp due to excess sebum
examples: The patient showed seborrheal symptoms with an oily scalp.
Seborrheal dermatitis can cause flaking and redness.

senses.definition: plural of seborrhea; conditions with excessive oily secretions of the skin or scalp
examples: The doctor diagnosed several seborrheas in different areas of the scalp.
Seborrheas can lead to dandruff and itching.

senses.definition: relating to seborrhea; characterized by excessive oiliness of the skin or scalp
examples: Seborrheic dermatitis is common on the scalp.
She has seborrheic symptoms, including flaky skin.

senses.definition: a condition of excessive oiliness of the skin or scalp; seborrhea
examples: Seborrhoea can cause persistent dandruff.
Treatment for seborrhoea may include medicated shampoos.

senses.definition: relating to seborrhoea; having characteristics of oily skin or scalp
examples: A seborrhoeal crust formed on the scalp.
Seborrhoeal conditions require medical attention.

senses.definition: plural of seborrhoea; conditions with excessive oiliness of the skin or scalp
examples: The patient reported several seborrhoeas on the face and scalp.
Seborrhoeas can be managed with regular cleansing.

senses.definition: related to seborrhoea; oily skin or scalp condition
examples: Seborrhoeic dermatitis causes red, flaky patches.
She has seborrhoeic areas on the scalp.

senses.definition: alternate spelling of seborrhoic; relating to seborrhoea
examples: Seborrhoic symptoms include oily skin and flaking.
The seborrhoic area was treated with a medicated shampoo.

senses.definition: a surname or place name; may also refer to a Serbo‑French vehicle? (note: uncommon; primarily a proper noun)
examples: The author Esan Sebright wrote under a pen name for privacy.
Sebright is a small village in the county.

senses.definition: an oily substance produced by skin glands to keep skin and hair moisturized
examples: Sebum moisturizes the skin and hair.
Excess sebum can lead to acne.

senses.definition: plural of sebum
examples: Sebums vary in amount across different skin regions.
Healthy skin secretes small amounts of sebum.

senses.definition: slang/affectionate term for young women with a sebum‑rich appearance? (informal, regionally used)
examples: The club featured a group of ebullient sebundies.
Sebundies admired their glossy hair.

senses.definition: alternative spelling of sebundy; a slang term (informal) for a fashionable young person, often with gloss or style
examples: A street sebundy walked by with styled hair.
The sebundy crowd gathered near the stage.

senses.definition: a short period of time; a moment
examples: Wait a sec while I grab my coat.
She completed the task in a few seconds.

senses.definition: SEC stands for United States Securities and Exchange Commission or other organizations; capitalized form used as an acronym
examples: The SEC regulates the stock market in the US.
She works at the SEC.

senses.definition: the quality or ability to be secured; the capacity to be secured or made secure
examples: The secability of the contract is questionable.
Technological secability improves with standards.

senses.definition: capable of being secured or cut? (ambiguous; likely rare/technical)
examples: The material is secable under high pressure.
Secable components can be separated easily.

senses.definition: a genus of grain plants including rye; a cereal used for flour
examples: Secale cereale is commonly known as rye.
Bread made from secale has a distinctive flavor.

senses.definition: a gluten protein found in rye (Secale cereale); part of secalin complex
examples: Secalin can cause problems for people with gluten sensitivity.
The rye secalin content is high in this variety.

senses.definition: perhaps an alternate spelling or related term to secalin; typically used in scientific contexts
examples: Secaline measurements were analyzed in the study.
Researchers examined secaline fractions.

senses.definition: a disaccharide sugar found in rye; a component of gluten-related compounds
examples: Secalose is studied for its role in rye digestion.
The secalose content affects flour quality.

senses.definition: plural of secalose
examples: Different rye varieties have varying secaloses.
The study measured several secaloses.

senses.definition: a plant, Secamone; also a genus in Apocynaceae used in medicine or dye
examples: Extracts from secamone have traditional uses.
Botanists study Secamone species.

senses.definition: the state or condition of being secant? (likely a misspelling or rare term; general meaning unclear)
examples: The term secancy appeared in the old manuscript.
Researchers noted secancy in the geometric context.

senses.definition: a line that cuts across a circle, intersecting it at two points
examples: The secant intersects the circle at two points.
In trigonometry, secant is 1 divided by cosine.

senses.definition: the secant of an angle; reciprocal of cosine
examples: cos θ is the adjacent over hypotenuse, while sec θ is 1/cos θ.
The secant function grows large near 90 degrees.

senses.definition: in a secant manner; with relation to or along a secant line (rare/technical)
examples: The curve progressed secantly as the graph was drawn.
Secantly, the line crossed the circle.
